title: 9 Effective Herbal Essential Oils for Treating Palpitation

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for palpitations because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to slow down an irregular heartbeat and promote a sense of relaxation and reduced stress.
Valeranone is good for palpitations because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to slow down heart rate and reduce feelings of anxiety and stress.
Isovaleric acid is good for palpitations because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to slow down a rapid heart rate and reduce anxiety.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oil, also known as valerian oil, is a natural remedy that can help with palpitations. This oil is extracted from the root of the valerian plant, which has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system. When inhaled, the oil can help slow down the heart rate and promote relaxation, reducing the sensation of palpitations. The oil contains compounds like valerenic acid, which has a sedative effect on the body, and linalool, which can help lower blood pressure. By reducing stress and anxiety, valerian oil can help alleviate palpitations caused by emotional factors. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce palpitations triggered by physical factors, such as inflammation or cardiovascular issues. Using valerian oil through inhalation or topical application may provide relief from palpitations by promoting a calm and peaceful state of mind and body.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oils that help with palpitations, start by harvesting fresh and clean Anthemis nobilis flowers in the early morning. You will need a clean glass jar, a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, and a clean cheesecloth or coffee filter. First, gently place the flowers into the glass jar and pour the carrier oil over them, making sure that the flowers are completely covered. Then, store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day to help the oil infuse into the flowers. After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the used flowers. Your Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oil is now ready to use. You can apply a few drops of this oil to your pulse points or inhale it to help calm your palpitations and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for palpitation because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can cause abnormal heartbeats and palpitations.
Kaempferol is good for palpitations because it acts as a calcium channel blocker, which helps to slow down heart rate and reduce the force of heart contractions.
Tannins are good for palpitations because they have a calming effect on the body, reducing anxiety and stress that can cause irregular heartbeats and palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as Passionflower, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help calm the nervous system. Its essential oil has been found to be effective in treating palpitations, which are irregular heartbeats that can be caused by stress, anxiety, or other health conditions. The oil contains compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids that have a soothing effect on the nervous system, helping to slow down heart rate and reduce anxiety. When inhaled, the essential oil can help calm the mind and body, reducing the frequency and intensity of palpitations. Additionally, the oil has a sedative effect, which can help individuals fall asleep and improve the quality of sleep, further reducing the risk of palpitations. By reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting relaxation, Passiflora incarnata essential oil can help alleviate palpitations and promote overall heart health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oil, also known as chamomile oil, which can help with palpitations, you will need dried chamomile flowers and a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil. First, fill a clean glass jar with dried chamomile flowers, making sure they are completely covered. Next, pour the carrier oil over the flowers, making sure the flowers are completely submerged. Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract from the flowers. After the extraction period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oil is now ready to use. To use it, add a few drops to a warm bath or inhale it directly from a cloth or handkerchief to help calm your heart rate and reduce palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for palpitation because it helps to reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to irregular heartbeat, by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.
Linalyl acetate is good for palpitations because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to slow down the heart rate and reduce anxiety, leading to a decrease in palpitations.
Limonene is good for palpitations because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to slow down a rapid heartbeat and promote relaxation.

Caryophyllene oxide is good for palpitation because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the heart and reduce stress and anxiety, which are often causes of irregular heartbeat and palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oil, also known as English lavender oil, has been shown to help with palpitations due to its calming effects on the nervous system. When inhaled, the oil sends a signal to the brain, which then helps to slow down the heart rate and reduce stress levels. This is because the oil contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a soothing effect on the body and mind. Palpitations often occur due to anxiety or stress, so by reducing these feelings, the oil can help to alleviate palpitations. Additionally, the oil's ability to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation can also contribute to its heart-calming effects. As the body and mind relax, the heart rate returns to a normal level, and palpitations subside.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oil that helps with palpitations, start by harvesting fresh leaves and flowers of the chamomile plant, also known as German chamomile, during the morning or late afternoon when the oil content is at its highest. Next, dry the harvested chamomile leaves and flowers in a warm, dark place, or using a dehydrator at a low temperature to preserve their natural properties. Once the leaves and flowers are completely dry, crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder. Transfer the powdered chamomile to a glass container and cover it with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or grapeseed oil in a 1:5 ratio. Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the container every day to allow the oil to infuse into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil. Finally, transfer the Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oil to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and store it in a cool, dark place, ready for use as a natural remedy for palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Luteolin is good for palpitations because it helps to relax the heart muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow, all of which can help to calm an irregular heartbeat.
Rosmarinic acid is good for palpitation because it has a calming effect on the heart and blood vessels, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can cause irregular heartbeat.

Apigenin is good for palpitations because it has a calming effect on the body, reduces anxiety and stress, and helps to regulate heart rhythms, thereby relieving symptoms of palpitations and promoting overall heart health.
Quercetin is good for palpitations because it helps to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation in the heart, which in turn can help to lower heart rate and reduce feelings of anxiety and stress.
Tannins are good for palpitation because they have a calming effect on the body, which helps to slow down the heart rate and reduce the occurrence of irregular heartbeats.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oil, also known as lemon balm, is a natural remedy that can help with palpitations. This is because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to slow down a rapid heartbeat. The oil contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate that have a sedative and anti-anxiety effect, making it easier to relax and reduce stress. When we're stressed, our heart rate increases, and palpitations can occur. By reducing stress and anxiety, Melissa officinalis oil can help to regulate the heartbeat and prevent palpitations. It can also help to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by palpitations, and this can further contribute to a reduction in palpitations.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oil, also known as chamomile oil, which can help with palpitations, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by gathering a large quantity of fresh chamomile flowers, either from your garden or a local organic farm. Next, carefully dry the flowers using a food dehydrator or by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels and letting them air dry for several days. Once the flowers are completely dry, add them to a large glass jar and cover them with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or grapeseed oil. Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two, allowing the oil to infuse with the chamomile's calming properties. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a smaller glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your homemade chamomile oil is now ready to use, either as a calming massage oil or added to a diffuser to promote relaxation and reduce palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for palpitation because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce stress and anxiety that can cause irregular heartbeats.

Shogaol in ANTHEMIS NOBILIS is good for palpitation because it has anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stress and calm the heart, which can help prevent or manage palpitations.
Zingiberene is good for palpitation because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as ginger oil, is often used to help people with palpitations. This is because ginger oil has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the body. When the heart is experiencing palpitations, it can cause inflammation in the body, which ginger oil can help to calm down. Additionally, ginger oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to slow down the heart rate and promote a sense of calm. Ginger oil also has a compound called gingerol, which has been shown to have anti-spasmodic properties, helping to ease the spasms in the heart that can cause palpitations. When inhaled or applied topically, ginger oil can help to bring balance to the body and promote a sense of calm, which can be beneficial for people experiencing palpitations.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oil that helps with palpitations, you'll need dried chamomile flowers, a clean glass jar, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a saucepan. First, fill the saucepan with a few inches of water and bring it to a boil. Next, place the clean glass jar in the saucepan, making sure the jar doesn't touch the water. Then, add a cup of dried chamomile flowers to the jar and cover it with a lid. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the chamomile flowers infuse in the hot air for about 30-40 minutes. After the infusion, carefully remove the jar from the saucepan and let the chamomile flowers cool down. Once cooled, strain the chamomile flowers from the oil using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the flowers. Finally, add a few drops of the infused oil to a carrier oil and mix well to create your Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oil. This oil can be used in aromatherapy to help calm palpitations and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for palpitations because they have a calming effect on the body, helping to lower blood pressure and reduce stress, which can cause irregular heartbeats and palpitations.
Saponins are good for palpitation because they help calm the nervous system and slow down heart rate, which can reduce feelings of anxiety and irregular heartbeat.
Ferulic acid is good for palpitation because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common causes of irregular heartbeat and palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oil, also known as oat oil, is a natural remedy for palpitations. It helps calm the nervous system and reduce stress, which are common causes of irregular heartbeat. The oil contains compounds that have a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. When applied topically or inhaled, it can slow down the heart rate and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate palpitations. Additionally, Avena sativa oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the heart and surrounding tissues, reducing inflammation that may be contributing to palpitations. By reducing stress and inflammation, Avena sativa oil can help regulate the heartbeat and promote a sense of calm, making it a potentially beneficial remedy for those experiencing palpitations. It's also known to help with sleep quality, which is often disrupted in people with palpitations.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oils that help with palpitations, first you need to dry and harvest fresh Anthemis nobilis flowers, also known as chamomile. Tie the flowers in small bunches and hang them upside down in a warm, dry, dark place until they are completely dry. After drying, you need to prepare a distillation apparatus, which typically consists of a still pot, condenser, and collecting vessel. Add the dried flowers to the still pot and heat the mixture using steam, allowing the essential oil to vaporize and rise through the condenser, where it will condense back into a liquid. The essential oil will then collect in the vessel below. This process is called steam distillation. Once the distillation is complete, you can filter the essential oil to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle to preserve its quality. Use the Anthemis nobilis essential oil as needed, ideally in a diffuser or diluted with a carrier oil, to help calm palpitations and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for palpitation because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help lower blood pressure and improve heart function, reducing the uncomfortable and irregular heartbeat.
Ginkgolide B is good for palpitations because it helps to reduce the occurrence of abnormal heart rhythms by blocking the action of certain chemicals in the body, thus promoting a stable heart rate.

Isorhapontigenin is good for palpitations because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of irregular heartbeats and palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oil is a natural remedy that can help with palpitations. It works by improving blood flow to the heart and relaxing the blood vessels. This helps to reduce the workload on the heart, allowing it to beat more steadily. Ginkgo biloba oil also contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to palpitations. Additionally, it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to slow down a rapid heartbeat. The oil's antioxidant properties can also help to protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and calming the nervous system, Ginkgo biloba essential oil may be able to help alleviate palpitations and promote a more regular heart rhythm.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oils, also known as chamomile oil, which can help with palpitations, start by harvesting fresh chamomile flowers from your garden or a local farm. Choose flowers with bright yellow centers and white petals. Next, dry the flowers completely either by air-drying them or by placing them in a low-temperature oven at 35-40°C (95-104°F) for 2-3 hours. Once the flowers are dry, place them in a clean glass jar and cover them with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, in a ratio of 1 part flowers to 4-5 parts oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. The resulting oil can be used as needed to help calm palpitations and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for palpitation because it helps to calm the nervous system and reduce stress, which in turn can slow down the heart rate and alleviate symptoms of palpitation.
Flavonoids are good for palpitation because they help reduce anxiety and stress by acting as a natural calmer and stabilizer of heart rhythms, thus promoting a healthy and regular heartbeat.

Liquiritin is good for palpitation because it helps to reduce the heart rate and promote relaxation by acting as a natural sedative and anti-anxiety agent, thereby alleviating the symptoms of palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil, also known as licorice root oil, is known to help with palpitations due to its calming and soothing properties. It has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can lead to irregular heartbeats. The oil also contains flavonoids and saponins, which are known to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing palpitations. Additionally, licorice root oil has been found to lower cortisol levels, the hormone responsible for stress, which can contribute to palpitations. By reducing stress and anxiety, licorice root oil can help regulate heart rhythms and alleviate palpitations naturally.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oils that help with palpitations, start by harvesting fresh chamomile flowers from a field or your garden. You'll need a large quantity of flowers, so plan accordingly. Next, dry the flowers completely in a warm, dark place, either by air-drying them or using a food dehydrator on a low setting. This process can take several days, so be patient. Once the flowers are dry, you can use a steam distiller to extract the essential oil. If you don't have access to a steam distiller, you can also use a solvent like jojoba oil to extract the oil from the flowers, although this method may not be as effective. After distillation, filter the essential oil to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle. To use the oil, dilute it with a carrier oil like jojoba oil and apply it topically to your wrists or temples, or inhale it through steam inhalation to help calm palpitations and promote relaxation.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Apigenin is good for palpitation because it has a calming effect on the body, which helps to slow down a fast heart rate and reduce anxiety that can cause palpitations.
Luteolin is good for palpitation because it has been shown to reduce inflammation and calm the heart by increasing the production of a protein that helps to regulate heart rhythm.
Quercetin is good for palpitations because it helps to calm the heart and reduce inflammation, which can cause irregular heartbeats and other symptoms of palpitations.
Rosmarinic acid is good for palpitation because it helps to calm the nervous system, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve blood flow, thereby regulating heart rate and reducing irregular heartbeat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Anthemis nobilis essential oil, also known as chamomile oil, is often used to help with palpitations due to its calming effects on the body. It contains compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which can act on the nervous system to reduce stress and anxiety. When we experience palpitations, it's often because our body is under a lot of stress, and our heart is beating rapidly as a result. Chamomile oil can help to calm the nervous system, which in turn can slow down the heart rate and help to alleviate palpitations. This oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the body, a common cause of palpitations. Additionally, chamomile oil can help to promote relaxation and reduce feelings of restlessness, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep, which is also beneficial for people experiencing palpitations.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Anthemis nobilis herbal essential oil, also known as chamomile essential oil, which helps with palpitations, you will need dried chamomile flowers and a distillation apparatus. First, carefully clean and dry the chamomile flowers to remove any dirt or moisture. Next, place the dried flowers into the distillation apparatus, making sure they are covered by the distillation water. Then, heat the water gently until it starts to boil, causing the water to turn into steam. The steam will then pass through the chamomile flowers, releasing their essential oils into the steam. Finally, the steam will be collected and cooled, resulting in a clear, fragrant essential oil that can be used to help calm palpitations and promote relaxation. It is essential to note that distillation should be done under the guidance of an experienced professional, as it requires careful attention to temperature and pressure to produce a safe and effective essential oil.

intro_desc: Palpitations, or irregular heartbeats, can be a distressing and uncomfortable experience. Using herbal essential oils, specifically valerian and passionflower, may help alleviate this issue. Valerian root oil, derived from Valeriana officinalis, has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate heart rhythms and reduce anxiety. This oil also contains sedative properties, making it easier to fall asleep, which is essential for maintaining a healthy heart. Passionflower oil, extracted from Passiflora incarnata, is known for its ability to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to palpitations. By inhaling these essential oils, you can help calm your nervous system and promote a more regular heartbeat.  Fixing the issue of palpitations is crucial for maintaining overall health and well-being. Palpitations can be a symptom of an underlying condition, such as anxiety or stress, which can further exacerbate the issue if left unaddressed. By addressing palpitations, you can improve your quality of life by reducing the frequency and severity of these episodes. This can be achieved by incorporating herbal essential oils, along with other natural remedies, into your daily routine. By doing so, you can reduce your reliance on medication and promote a more natural approach to maintaining heart health. Improving your life by addressing palpitations can also have a positive impact on your mental and emotional well-being, allowing you to feel more calm and centered.
supplementary_best: A combination of Passiflora incarnata (50%), Lavandula angustifolia (25%), and Valeriana officinalis (25%) may help remedy palpitations. Passiflora incarnata is known for its calming effects, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that may contribute to palpitations. Lavandula angustifolia has a soothing impact on the nervous system and can promote relaxation, which can also help alleviate palpitations. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural relaxant that can help slow down heart rate and reduce palpitations.  This combination is effective because it addresses the root cause of palpitations, which is often stress and anxiety. Passiflora incarnata and Lavandula angustifolia work together to calm the nervous system, while Valeriana officinalis helps slow down heart rate. This blend may provide a balanced approach to managing palpitations.
supplementary_avoid: If you have palpitations, it's best to avoid certain herbal essential oils that can trigger or worsen the condition. One oil to be cautious with is 'Eucalyptus globulus'. This oil can cause a rapid heartbeat and increase blood pressure due to its stimulating properties. Another oil to avoid is 'Peppermint (Mentha piperita)'. While peppermint oil can be calming, it can also stimulate the heart and worsen palpitations in some individuals. 'Rosemary (Rosmarinus officinalis)' oil should also be used with caution, as it can increase heart rate and blood pressure due to its stimulating effects. Additionally, 'Thyme (Thymus vulgare)' oil can have a similar effect, causing an increase in heart rate and blood pressure. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal essential oils, especially if you have a pre-existing heart condition or are experiencing palpitations.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel can help calm palpitations by reducing inflammation and stress on the heart. The gel contains compounds like vitamins A, C, and E, as well as amino acids and minerals, which work together to soothe the heart and promote relaxation. Drinking aloe vera juice can also help lower blood pressure and heart rate, contributing to a more stable heartbeat.  Another natural remedy for palpitations is apple cider vinegar. The acidity in apple cider vinegar helps to stabilize the heartbeat by promoting the production of potassium, an essential mineral that helps regulate heart rhythm. Additionally, the acetic acid in apple cider vinegar has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress on the heart and promote overall cardiovascular health.  Ginger has also been found to be beneficial in managing palpitations. The active compound in ginger, gingerol, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to palpitations. Ginger also helps to relax the blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, which can help to calm the heartbeat.  Ashwagandha, an herb commonly used in Ayurvedic medicine, can also help to reduce palpitations. Ashwagandha is an adaptogen, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress, which can contribute to palpitations. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, ashwagandha can help to calm the heartbeat and promote overall cardiovascular health.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience palpitations may also have other health issues. One common problem is high blood pressure. Palpitations can be a sign that blood pressure is too high, and medicinal herbs like hawthorn and passionflower can help lower it. Hawthorn helps relax blood vessels and improves blood flow, while passionflower calms the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can cause blood pressure to rise.  Another issue people with palpitations may face is anxiety and stress. Herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng can help manage these conditions. Ashwagandha reduces cortisol levels, a hormone that increases when we're stressed, while ginseng improves mood and reduces anxiety.  Some people with palpitations may also experience insomnia or difficulty sleeping. Herbs like valerian root and lemon balm can help with this. Valerian root promotes relaxation and calms the mind, making it easier to fall asleep, while lemon balm reduces anxiety and stress, leading to a restful night's sleep.  Furthermore, palpitations can be a symptom of an underlying heart condition. Herbs like turmeric and ginger can help alleviate these conditions. Turmeric reduces inflammation, which can help prevent heart disease, while ginger improves blood flow and reduces blood pressure.  Lastly, people with palpitations may experience fatigue and low energy. Herbs like ginkgo biloba and cayenne pepper can help with this. Ginkgo biloba improves blood flow and reduces inflammation, while cayenne pepper increases circulation and energy levels.  These medicinal herbs work by interacting with the body's natural systems, such as the nervous and circulatory systems. They can help alleviate symptoms and address the underlying causes of palpitations.
